description:
Trigger: user provisions an Ed25519 key in their HSM (CKK_EC_EDWARDS in PKCS#11 v3.0+), points secretx-pkcs11 at it, calls .sign().

Maintenance consequence: secretx_core::SigningAlgorithm has Ed25519 as a public variant (secretx-core/src/lib.rs:1296). Users will reasonably expect that if their HSM supports Ed25519 and the SigningAlgorithm enum includes it, secretx-pkcs11 supports it. It does not — pkcs11_detect_algorithm at lib.rs:271-289 only matches KeyType::EC and KeyType::RSA, returning 'unsupported key type: <kt>' for everything else. README claims 'compatible with any PKCS#11 v2.40-compliant token' which is true at the protocol level but misleading at the algorithm level.

This will produce a recurring class of issue reports from users with Ed25519 keys. Two fixes:
(a) implement Ed25519: cryptoki has KeyType::EC_EDWARDS; the mechanism is CKM_EDDSA. detect_ec_algorithm needs to also accept Ed25519 OID (1.3.101.112) and return SigningAlgorithm::Ed25519. The sign match arm needs a SigningAlgorithm::Ed25519 => session.sign(&Mechanism::Eddsa, ...) branch.
(b) document loudly in README that only EC P-256 and RSA-2048-PSS are supported in this version; document the algorithm matrix.

(a) is preferable because Ed25519 in HSMs is increasingly common (YubiHSM2 firmware ≥2.2, recent SoftHSM2, HSMs targeting Web3).
title: Ed25519/EC-Edwards keys silently rejected with vague error despite SigningAlgorithm::Ed25519 in API
